name: Student
description: Stay on top of coursework, money, and the early career build. Domains for learning, finances, calendar, and career.

domains.title: Learning
domains.soul:
# Learning

Courses, deadlines, and what you actually understand vs. what you have only seen. Turn lectures and readings into durable knowledge, not just notes.
domains.goals:
# Goals

- Never miss an assignment deadline
- Review hard topics before exams, not the night before
- Keep a running list of concepts I do not yet understand
prompts: What assignments and exams are due in the next two weeks, ranked by urgency?
Quiz me on the concepts I marked as not-yet-understood.
Turn my notes from this week into a short study summary.

domains.title: Money
domains.soul:
# Money

Tuition, loans, income, and spending on a tight budget. Build good habits now: track where it goes, avoid avoidable debt, start saving even small amounts.
domains.goals:
# Goals

- Stay within a monthly budget
- Understand my loans and their terms
- Start an emergency buffer, however small
prompts: Help me build a realistic monthly budget from my income and fixed costs.
Explain my student loans in plain language: balance, rate, and when payments start.

domains.title: Calendar
domains.soul:
# Calendar

Classes, deadlines, work shifts, and a life outside of all of it. Keep the schedule realistic and protect time to actually study.
domains.goals:
# Goals

- See the week ahead every Sunday
- Protect study blocks before big deadlines
- Avoid scheduling conflicts between work and class
prompts: Give me a preview of this week: classes, deadlines, and study blocks.
Find conflicts between my work shifts and my coursework this week.

domains.title: Career
domains.soul:
# Career

The early build: internships, skills, a portfolio, and the network you start now. Small consistent steps compound over four years.
domains.goals:
# Goals

- Apply to internships before deadlines
- Build one portfolio-worthy project per term
- Reach out to one new contact each month
prompts: What should I be doing this term to be ready for internship applications?
Help me draft an outreach message to someone working in a role I want.
